steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with a noun phrase—”A nearly frictionless state”—followed by a comma, marking an appositive.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
An appositive at the start of a sentence must be immediately followed by the noun it describes, which serves as the subject of the main clause. “A nearly frictionless state” describes “superlubricity,” so the blank must provide this noun as the subject of an independent clause.

D does this: superlubricity is reached when two surfaces' COF drops below 0.01.
  • A places a dependent clause before the noun
  • B introduces a second noun phrase
  • C presents a gerund phrase instead
.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
